title: Advanced Graves
description: The Best Advanced Graves Mod/Datapack with Highly Customizable Features!
body:
![Advanced-Graves](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/a74aff7f02b73b7119c67bcd330c81ab06bae094.png) -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ![Introduction](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/19adf4385c1d70342aa7571504d8833eceb70791_0.webp) Tired of dying in lava, the void, or a brutal PVP fight only to watch your gear scatter and despawn forever? Say goodbye to frustration and hello to smart, safe, and highly customizable death protection! **SnapGraves** creates a secure grave at your death location that stores every single item and all your experience – exactly as they were in your inventory. No more random drops, no more racing against despawn timers in dangerous spots. -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ![Features](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/ba1afd14f4e061644a9802c89e8c69c22e149e3a_0.webp) **Multi-Version Compatible** Our **Advanced Graves Datapack** is compatible with **both datapacks and mods** for **Fabric**, **Forge**, **Neoforge** and **Quilt** from **1.21.6 to 1.21.11**. **Fully Configurable via In-Game Menu** No editing files needed! **Adjust xp drop %**, **toggle remote looting/deleting**, **grave limit**, **grave glowing**, and more with easy toggles, sliders, and options with no commands! Only settgins menu! **No Resource Pack Required** Download and play. That's it! **Multiplayer & SMP Friendly** Graves respect **ownership**, protection bubbles, to **prevent griefing**. # Grave Spawning Your grave will spawn exactly the block you died. Graves are invulnerable, no hitbox, and with low level of light. Meaning that you can easily walk through graves and find the grave at night time. Graves can also set to be glowing, so you can see them through walls. By default, graves glowing is turned off, you can easily toggle glowing in menu if you are the server admin.   After a grave spawns, The exact location of your grave will be announced to you: ![Death Message](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/8e5ede179c954a1dd944635c2c6fe9e3c67efd2d.webp) **Our Graves Datapack Support Custom Dimension Death Message!** The death message format is: ``` [Death icon][Player Head][Player Name][Grave ID]||[Death Location]||[Death Dimension] ``` # Grave Opening When you back to the death location, you will see your grave. The grave with mossy stone texture, along with a text display showing your skin head, your player name and your grave's ID, with the days you survived in the world. | Grave in Game | Model | | :---: | :---:| | ![Grave](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/0d4f023b84b461f5770cf6a2b74167e358ae2238.png) | ![Model](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/49ed904c45fd297ed7d7c0a339e27fce3aecb4c5_0.webp) | You can claim the grave by **both left/right click**, soul particles and sound will be displayed. Items and experience orbs will drop to the ground for you to pick them up. The graves do not disappear until the owner claims it. **Experience orbs drop percentage can be configurated in the config menu by admin, which will be shown in the next section.** | Before | After | | :---: | :---:| |![Claim GIF](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/fb296a7d99fec516b3c9ff08336f819824268eb0.gif)| ![Claim Inventory](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/a2cdc910b957d3c55f501f82f3886f9b5cf05e8a.png)| Tips: All the graves are invulnerable, which means no TNTs or creepers can destroy your graves!❤️ # Graves Display Menu Our **Andvanced Grave Datapack** has an menu for player to track their graves. Simply press `G` key to open the menu and you will see a menu pop up. **⚠️If the menu did not show up, please check `quick actions` key in your game keybind settings.⚠️** Inside the menu, there shows the graves ID, coordinate of graves, dimension of graves, remote looting function button, and remote deleting function button. The graves are displayed in decending order of graves ID. And, of course, the garves menu also **support custom dimension** display. ![Graves Display Menu](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/5953749d35bf423fa821437beb639856feb10746.png) **Remote looting** lets you to get back all your items and equipments of that grave with no distance restriction and dimension restriction. You can loot your grave everywhere! Dispite remote looting, **remote deleting** lets you to delete the grave without getting back the items. If you die with full inventory of trash, you may need this. | Remote Looting | Remote Deleting | | :---: | :---:| |![Remote Looting](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/de2df1292482dad8aeb8b494e88be9fae0e7568c.gif)| ![Remote Deleting](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/0bbae5970f311dada0b2c117a12d623aadfc6b5c.gif)| -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ![CONFIG](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/0e89d13f0adb97a7151723230ceafe15590dc1b3_0.webp) **Server Admin** can config the **Advanced Graves Datapack** by entering this command: ``` /function snapgrave:settings/menu ``` You will see this screen pop up instantly: | Congfig Menu | Config Notes | | :---: | :---:| |![Config Menu](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/c4c9017d9949c86ec3d6816f8b1cc0f3a1270850.png)| ![Config Notes](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/c87196a2a6fa20fb4ab069f75fd5bdabf73fbdf3.png)| In this config menu, you can customize: **XP Drops %** The amount of xp points that player can retrieve after claiming the grave. **Grave Limit Per Player** The maximum grave that player can spawn after death, the oldest grave will be overwritten if exceed. **Remote Looting** Enable/Disble the remote looting function. **Remote Deleting** Enable/Diable the remote deleting function. **Glowing** Enable/Disable the glowing effect of graves. **⚠️NOTES TO READ BEFORE CONFIGURATING⚠️** 1. The settings are preset with the current data. 2. The Delete option is available only when the Loot option is enabled. 3. The Grave limit is automatically disabled if Immediate Respaun is turned on. -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ![FAQ](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/a8c39092f41efd6ee45c4f242b169399a5576082_0.webp)
Will my items disappear when I die? No. The moment you die, all your items and equipment are safely locked into a gravestone at your death location. Nothing drops on the ground. As long as you find and claim your grave, everything will be perfectly preserved.
Can gravestones spawn in the Nether or End? Yes. SnapGrave works seamlessly across all three dimensions. Your exact death location is recorded, and the grave info displays which dimension it's in, so you know exactly where to go.
If I die again before claiming my first grave, will both gravestones remain? Yes. Each death creates an independent gravestone. You can view all your graves through the "My Graves" list with their locations, and claim them one by one.
Can other players open my grave and take my stuff? No. Each gravestone is bound to its creator. Only you can claim it. Other players cannot interact with your grave even if they're standing right next to it.
My grave is in a dangerous location and I'm afraid to go back. What can I do? Server admins can enable "Remote Looting," allowing you to retrieve items directly from the graves list without physically traveling back to the death location.
Will my accumulated experience be preserved after death? Yes. SnapGrave records your XP when you die and returns it when you claim the grave (return percentage is adjustable by admins, default 100%).
How do I know where my grave is? What if I forget where I died? The chat displays exact coordinates and dimension immediately after death. Additionally, you can open the "My Graves" menu anytime to view all your grave locations—you'll never lose track.
If I have many unclaimed graves, will they fill up and prevent new ones from spawning? Admins can set a grave limit per player. When approaching the limit, you'll receive an advance warning. If exceeded, the oldest grave is automatically removed to ensure your latest death always has a grave.
What does the gravestone look like? Can I tell which one is mine? Yes. The gravestone displays your player head and name above it, making it instantly recognizable. You won't confuse whose grave is whose when you return.
How do I open my graves list? Click the Quick Actions menu in the top-right corner of your screen and select "My Graves." No commands needed—fully GUI-based operation.
Will my graves persist after server restarts? Yes. Grave data is stored in the server's persistent storage. Restarts or datapack reloads won't affect them until you personally claim them.
Can gravestones spawn in lava or the void? Yes. Gravestones spawn at the exact death coordinates (including void coordinates). However, for lava or void deaths, we recommend using "Remote Looting" to retrieve items directly from the list, avoiding further danger.
Is there any feedback when claiming a grave? How do I know it worked? Upon successful claim, the grave plays a soul escape sound and releases particle effects for visual and audio confirmation. Your items and XP appear directly on you—nothing drops on the ground.
Can I delete old graves I don't want anymore? Yes. In the Graves list, each grave has a delete button next to it. You can remove specific graves individually without affecting others.
Will this datapack interfere with normal PvP or survival gameplay? No. SnapGrave only intervenes at the moment of death to store items and create the grave. Otherwise, it doesn't interfere with normal gameplay. Graves don't affect terrain or buildings.
What settings can admins adjust? Admins can configure: XP return percentage (0–100%), grave limit per player, remote looting toggle, remote deletion toggle, and grave glowing effect. All settings take effect immediately without server restart.
With "Remote Looting" enabled, can anyone remotely take others' graves? No. Remote looting still respects grave ownership. Only the grave owner can operate it—other players cannot remotely access someone else's items.
What is the Glowing feature? When enabled, all gravestones emit a glowing aura effect, making them easier to spot in dark environments. No need to search with torches.

Thank you!| # Support Me! Your Support Helps Me To Survive.
